summary: Google shipped Chrome Skills, a new Gemini-powered browser feature that turns reusable prompts into one-click workflows across selected tabs, extending AI automation directly inside Chrome. ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM for more natural real-time voice interaction, while AWS made the Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore generally available to speed enterprise agent deployment. On the research side, the paper "Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models" offers a potentially important recipe for improving post-training efficiency, though it remains early-stage academic work.

items.title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
items.summary: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

items.title: Google introduces "Skills" in Chrome to make Gemini prompts instantly reusable
items.summary: You can save custom prompts you find useful or grab a premade Skill from Google's library.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Google AI Blog announced a Chrome capability that turns AI prompts into one-click tools. The post presents this feature through a video showing Skills in Chrome.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: Google is productizing browser-native AI automation inside Chrome, signaling a distribution advantage for Gemini in everyday enterprise and consumer workflows.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Google Chrome Skills Launches Gemini-Powered Reusable Prompt Workflows
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: Chrome desktop users can save prompts as "Skills" and run them repeatedly across multiple selected tabs, turning ad hoc Gemini usage into standardized browser workflows.
Google is pairing custom user-created Skills with a prebuilt Skill library, which lowers setup friction and could accelerate adoption beyond power users.
The feature extends Gemini's integration inside Chrome rather than requiring a separate app, giving Google a direct path to embed AI task automation into a high-frequency product.

top_topics.title: Introducing Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
top_topics.summary: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a speech model designed for attentive listening and interference suppression. The model is positioned for more natural interactive voice conversations.

items.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment Opens
items.summary: ByteDance Seed announced that its 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment program is now open, including internship opportunities.

items.title: Introducing Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
items.summary: ByteDance Seed announced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a speech model designed for attentive listening, robust interference suppression, and more natural interactive conversations.

items.title: Seed 2.0 Official Launch
items.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0, marking the release of its new model generation.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a speech model designed for attentive listening and interference suppression. The model is positioned for more natural interactive voice conversations.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: A full-duplex speech model from ByteDance points to intensifying competition in low-latency voice AI, an area with major implications for assistants, customer support, and multimodal agents.
top_topics.HEADLINE: ByteDance Seed Unveils Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM for Real-Time Voice Interaction
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: ByteDance Seed says Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM is designed for attentive listening, robust interference suppression, and more natural interactive conversations.
The release suggests ByteDance is investing in simultaneous listen-and-speak voice systems rather than turn-based speech pipelines, a key capability for human-like conversational agents.
ByteDance also surfaced Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, indicating an active model release cadence around both speech and foundation-model evaluation.

top_topics.summary: Ars Technica reported on Mythos AI testing by the UK government and said the model was the first AI system to complete a difficult multistep infiltration challenge. The article presents the tests as evidence separating concrete cybersecurity capability from hype.

top_topics.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
top_topics.summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succeeds or fails: (i) the student and teacher should share compatible thinking patterns; and (ii) even with consistent thinking patterns and higher scores, the teacher must offer genuinely new capabilities beyond what the student has seen during training. We validate these findings through weak-to-stron...

items.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
items.summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succeeds or fails: (i) the student and teacher should share compatible thinking patterns; and (ii) even with consistent thinking patterns and higher scores, the teacher must offer genuinely new capabilities beyond what the student has seen during training. We validate these findings through weak-to-stron...

metadata.ai_summary: On-policy distillation dynamics in large language models depend on compatible thinking patterns between teacher and student models, with successful distillation characterized by alignment on high-probability tokens and requiring teachers to provide novel capabilities beyond student training data.
metadata.spotlight_comment: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succe...

items.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
items.summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succeeds or fails: (i) the student and teacher should share compatible thinking patterns; and (ii) even with consistent thinking patterns and higher scores, the teacher must offer genuinely new capabilities beyond what the student has seen during training. We validate these findings through weak-to-stron...

metadata.comment: Systematically studies on-policy distillation dynamics, identifying when teacher-student compatibility and novelty determine success or failure.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succeeds or fails: (i) the student and teacher should share compatible thinking patterns; and (ii) even with consistent thinking patterns and higher scores, the teacher must offer genuinely new capabilities beyond what the student has seen during training. We validate these findings through weak-to-stron...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: If validated, the paper could improve how frontier labs and model builders distill stronger models into cheaper deployable systems during post-training.
top_topics.HEADLINE: "Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models" Proposes a New OPD Training Recipe
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The paper studies on-policy distillation, a core post-training method for LLMs, and argues that its success depends on two governing conditions rather than simple scaling alone.
Its contribution is mechanistic and prescriptive: it aims to explain OPD dynamics and provide a practical recipe for when distillation should work reliably.
Because the work is currently circulating via arXiv and paper aggregators rather than broad independent replication, it is more relevant for research roadmaps than immediate product decisions.

top_topics.title: Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is now Generally Available
top_topics.summary: AWS announced general availability of the Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. The open-source SDK is intended to help developers build production-ready AI agents and run them on AgentCore Runtime.

items.title: Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is now Generally Available
items.summary: With the new Spring AI AgentCore SDK, you can build production-ready AI agents and run them on the highly scalable AgentCore Runtime. The Spring AI AgentCore SDK is an open source library that brings Amazon Bedrock AgentCore capabilities into Spring AI. In this post, we build an AI agent starting with a chat endpoint, then adding streaming responses, conversation memory, and tools for web browsing and code execution.

items.title: Best practices to run inference on Amazon SageMaker HyperPod
items.summary: This post explores how Amazon SageMaker HyperPod provides a comprehensive solution for inference workloads. We walk you through the platform’s key capabilities for dynamic scaling, simplified deployment, and intelligent resource management. By the end of this post, you’ll understand how to use the HyperPod automated infrastructure, cost optimization features, and performance enhancements to reduce your total cost of ownership by up to 40% while accelerating your generative AI deployments from concept to production.

items.title: Use-case based deployments on SageMaker JumpStart
items.summary: We're excited to announce the launch of Amazon SageMaker JumpStart optimized deployments. SageMaker JumpStart improved deployments address the need for rich and straightforward deployment customization on SageMaker JumpStart by offering pre-defined deployment configurations, designed for specific use cases. Customers maintain the same level of visibility into the details of their proposed deployments, but now deployments are optimized for their specific use case and performance constraint.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: AWS announced general availability of the Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. The open-source SDK is intended to help developers build production-ready AI agents and run them on AgentCore Runtime.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: AWS is reducing enterprise adoption friction for agentic AI by connecting Bedrock AgentCore directly into the widely used Spring developer ecosystem.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Reaches General Availability
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The open-source Spring AI AgentCore SDK lets developers build production-ready AI agents in Spring AI and run them on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime.
General availability matters for enterprise buyers because Spring is deeply embedded in Java back-end stacks, making Bedrock easier to adopt without major architectural changes.
The launch strengthens AWS's position in enterprise agent infrastructure by tying model orchestration and runtime services to familiar application-development tooling.

topics.title: Zig 0.16.0 release notes: "Juicy Main"
topics.summary:
Zig 0.16.0 release notes: "Juicy Main"

Zig has really good release notes - comprehensive, detailed, and with relevant usage examples for each of the new features.

Of particular note in the newly released Zig 0.16.0 is what they are calling "Juicy Main" - a dependency injection feature for your program's main() function where accepting a process.Init parameter grants access to a struct of useful properties:

const std = @import("std"); p...

topics.title: Trusted access for the next era of cyber defense
topics.summary: OpenAI expanded its Trusted Access for Cyber program for vetted defenders. The update includes the introduction of GPT-5.4-Cyber and additional safeguards tied to advancing AI cybersecurity capabilities.

items.title: Trusted access for the next era of cyber defense
items.summary:
Trusted access for the next era of cyber defense

OpenAI's answer to Claude Mythos appears to be a new model called GPT-5.4-Cyber:

In preparation for increasingly more capable models from OpenAI over the next few months, we are fine-tuning our models specifically to enable defensive cybersecurity use cases, starting today with a variant of GPT‑5.4 trained to be cyber-permissive: GPT‑5.4‑Cyber.

topics.title: datasette PR #2689: Replace token-based CSRF with Sec-Fetch-Site header protection
topics.summary:
datasette PR #2689: Replace token-based CSRF with Sec-Fetch-Site header protection

Datasette has long protected against CSRF attacks using CSRF tokens, implemented using my asgi-csrf Python library. These are something of a pain to work with - you need to scatter forms in templates with <input type="hidden" name="csrftoken" value="{{ csrftoken() }}"> lines and then selectively disable CSRF protection for APIs that are intended to be called from outside the browser.

I've been following Filippo Valsorda's research here with interest, described in this detailed essay from August 2025 and...

topics.title: Latent Planning Emerges with Scale
topics.summary: LLMs can perform seemingly planning-intensive tasks, like writing coherent stories or functioning code, without explicitly verbalizing a plan; however, the extent to which they implicitly plan is unknown. In this paper, we define latent planning as occurring when LLMs possess internal planning representations that (1) cause the generation of a specific future token or concept, and (2) shape preceding context to license said future token or concept. We study the Qwen-3 family (0.6B-14B) on simple planning tasks, finding that latent planning ability increases with scale. Models that plan poss...

topics.title: A Bayesian Perspective on the Role of Epistemic Uncertainty for Delayed Generalization in In-Context Learning
topics.summary: In-context learning enables transformers to adapt to new tasks from a few examples at inference time, while grokking highlights that this generalization can emerge abruptly only after prolonged training. We study task generalization and grokking in in-context learning using a Bayesian perspective, asking what enables the delayed transition from memorization to generalization. Concretely, we consider modular arithmetic tasks in which a transformer must infer a latent linear function solely from in-context examples and analyze how predictive uncertainty evolves during training. We combine app...

topics.title: Aethon: A Reference-Based Replication Primitive for Constant-Time Instantiation of Stateful AI Agents
topics.summary: The transition from stateless model inference to stateful agentic execution is reshaping the systems assumptions underlying modern AI infrastructure. While large language models have made persistent, tool-using, and collaborative agents technically viable, existing runtime architectures remain constrained by materialization-heavy instantiation models that impose significant latency and memory overhead. This paper introduces Aethon, a reference-based replication primitive for near-constant-time instantiation of stateful AI agents. Rather than reconstructing agents as fully materialized objec...

topics.title: Algorithmic Analysis of Dense Associative Memory: Finite-Size Guarantees and Adversarial Robustness
topics.summary: Dense Associative Memory (DAM) generalizes Hopfield networks through higher-order interactions and achieves storage capacity that scales as $O(N^{n-1})$ under suitable pattern separation conditions. Existing dynamical analyses primarily study the thermodynamic limit $N\to\infty$ with randomly sampled patterns and therefore do not provide finite-size guarantees or explicit convergence rates. We develop an algorithmic analysis of DAM retrieval dynamics that yields finite-$N$ guarantees under explicit, verifiable pattern conditions. Under a separation assumption and a bounded-interference cond...

topics.title: Chain-of-Models Pre-Training: Rethinking Training Acceleration of Vision Foundation Models
topics.summary: In this paper, we present Chain-of-Models Pre-Training (CoM-PT), a novel performance-lossless training acceleration method for vision foundation models (VFMs). This approach fundamentally differs from existing acceleration methods in its core motivation: rather than optimizing each model individually, CoM-PT is designed to accelerate the training pipeline at the model family level, scaling efficiently as the model family expands. Specifically, CoM-PT establishes a pre-training sequence for the model family, arranged in ascending order of model size, called model chain. In this chain, only t...

metadata.comment: Accelerates training across an entire model family by chaining models and transferring parameters and features from smaller to larger pretrained models.

items.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
items.summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succeeds or fails: (i) the student and teacher should share compatible thinking patterns; and (ii) even with consistent thinking patterns and higher scores, the teacher must offer genuinely new capabilities beyond what the student has seen during training. We validate these findings through weak-to-stron...
items.spotlight_comment: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succe...

items.title: KnowRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning with Minimal-Sufficient Knowledge Guidance
items.summary: RLVR improves reasoning in large language models, but its effectiveness is often limited by severe reward sparsity on hard problems. Recent hint-based RL methods mitigate sparsity by injecting partial solutions or abstract templates, yet they typically scale guidance by adding more tokens, which introduce redundancy, inconsistency, and extra training overhead. We propose KnowRL (Knowledge-Guided Reinforcement Learning), an RL training framework that treats hint design as a minimal-sufficient guidance problem. During RL training, KnowRL decomposes guidance into atomic knowledge points (KPs)...
items.spotlight_comment: RLVR improves reasoning in large language models, but its effectiveness is often limited by severe reward sparsity on hard problems. Recent hint-based RL methods mitigate sparsity by injecting partial solutions or abstract templates, yet they typically scale guidance by adding more tokens, which...

items.title: Parcae: Scaling Laws For Stable Looped Language Models
items.summary: A new scalable language-model architecture recipe with explicit stability theory and scaling-law results makes it broadly important beyond niche interest.
items.spotlight_comment: A new scalable language-model architecture recipe with explicit stability theory and scaling-law results makes it broadly important beyond niche interest.

items.title: How Transformers Learn to Plan via Multi-Token Prediction
items.summary: Broadly important today: Shows multi-token prediction induces reverse-planning circuits in transformers through a cleaner gradient signal than next-token training.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Shows multi-token prediction induces reverse-planning circuits in transformers through a cleaner gradient signal than next-token training.

items.title: ZoomR: Memory Efficient Reasoning through Multi-Granularity Key Value Retrieval
items.summary: Broadly important today: Uses hierarchical summaries plus query-conditioned KV retrieval to reduce decoding memory for long reasoning generations by over 4x.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Uses hierarchical summaries plus query-conditioned KV retrieval to reduce decoding memory for long reasoning generations by over 4x.

items.title: VFA: Relieving Vector Operations in Flash Attention with Global Maximum Pre-computation
items.summary: Broadly important today: Reduces FlashAttention softmax vector bottlenecks by rethinking running-max updates and block traversal order.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Reduces FlashAttention softmax vector bottlenecks by rethinking running-max updates and block traversal order.
